title: DocumentComparisonResult
description: Schema for document comparison between extracted document and purchase order. Each result contains field-by-field comparisons with human-readable explanations.

FieldComparison.title: FieldComparison
FieldComparison.description: A single field comparison showing PO value, document value, and explanation

field.description: Human-readable field name displayed to user

poValue.description: Value from the Purchase Order, formatted for display. Use human-readable format (e.g., 'USD $28.00' not '28'). Use 'Not specified' if field is empty in PO.

documentValue.description: Value extracted from the document, formatted for display. Use human-readable format (e.g., 'CAD $28.00' not '28'). Use 'Not specified' if field is empty in document.

match.description: Match classification: 'exact' = identical or semantically equivalent, 'close' = minor differences (formatting, typos), 'mismatch' = significant difference requiring attention

isBlocking.description: True if this specific field comparison causes a blocking issue. Blocking fields include: price differences, currency mismatches, quantity discrepancies, items not in PO, oversupply.

explanation.description: For exact matches, use empty string or brief 'Exact match'. For close/mismatch, provide a complete, detailed human-readable sentence explaining the difference. Must be easy to read and understand. Do NOT include 'BLOCKING:' prefix or match scores.
examples: The address uses a slightly different format but refers to the same location.
The brand name 'Cursor' on the invoice corresponds to the legal company name 'Anysphere, Inc.' on the purchase order.
The email address contains a typo with an extra letter 'i', showing 'hii@cursor.com' instead of 'hi@cursor.com'.
The purchase order specifies USD currency but the invoice is billed in CAD. This requires review as currency conversion may affect the final amount.
The unit price on the invoice is $5.00 higher than what was agreed on the purchase order.
The invoice quantity of 8 units exceeds the purchase order quantity of 5 units by 3 units.
This line item appears on the invoice but was not included in the original purchase order.
The street address is missing the unit number 434 that appears on the purchase order.

ComparisonResult.title: ComparisonResult
ComparisonResult.description: Result of comparing a single document part (vendor, ship-to, or line item) against the purchase order

type.description: Type of entity being compared: 'vendor' = supplier/seller, 'ship_to' = delivery address, 'line_item' = individual product/service

status.description: Comparison status for this part based on matchScore: 'matched' (>0.9), 'partial' (0.5-0.9), 'unmatched' (<0.5)

matchScore.description: Overall match score calculated from individual field comparisons. 1.0 = perfect match, 0.0 = complete mismatch

severity.description: Severity level: 'info' = cosmetic differences, 'warning' = notable differences, 'blocking' = critical issues that prevent approval

extractedIndex.description: Zero-based index of the line item in the extracted document (only for type='line_item')

poIndex.description: Zero-based index of the matching line item in the PO, or null if no match found (only for type='line_item')

comparisons.description: Field-by-field comparisons with human-readable values and explanations. Each entry shows exactly what was compared and why.

documentId.description: Unique identifier for the extracted document (e.g., 'mg-1769309498742-ceee7536')

poId.description: Display ID of the purchase order being compared against (e.g., '2505007')

overallStatus.description: Overall comparison status: 'matched' = all fields match, 'partial' = some fields match, 'unmatched' = critical mismatches found

confidence.description: Overall confidence in the comparison result (0.0 = no confidence, 1.0 = fully confident)

blockingIssues.description: Count of results with severity='blocking'. These are critical issues that must be resolved before approval.

results.description: Array of comparison results - one for vendor, one for ship_to, and one per line item
